text.title: Architecture Workflows Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every workflow-instance must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: workflow_type MUST be one of {system-design, architecture-review, adr, tech-eval, atam-cbam, strangler-migration, design-doc-review}. Custom or unnamed workflows are invalid; if a trigger does not match, use SDD.

rationale.source: SEI ATAM/CBAM evaluation methods + Strangler Fig pattern (Fowler)

rule.rationale: Naming the workflow constrains the step list and the review criteria; ad-hoc workflows leak ceremony.

rule.statement: Every step MUST name at least one of {clarifier, designer, critic, documenter, stakeholder}. Steps without a named role are invalid; "team" is not a role.

rationale.source: Internal — workflow audit on 12 architecture activities 2025

rule.rationale: Steps without a named owner stall; the named role creates accountability and lets sub-agents specialise.

rule.statement: Every step MUST emit a named artefact (file path + format) on disk before the next step starts. In-memory-only steps are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Materialised state survives subagent context loss and lets reviewers verify the step output independently.

rule.statement: Any step that produces a type-1 (irreversible) decision MUST have a review-gate=true flag and a named reviewer. Decisions cannot self-approve.
rule.rationale: Type-1 decisions without review compound architectural risk; the review gate is the only catch before the decision becomes durable.

rule.statement: Every workflow MUST declare an abort condition (observable signal that the workflow is no longer applicable). Workflows without an exit door run forever.
rule.rationale: Stuck workflows consume cycles indefinitely; the abort condition lets the team recognise sunk-cost early.
